title: Resonant Cavity Interferometry
content:
Juno Langford led a research group at the Ironquay Laboratory Consortium in Frosthaven that advanced resonant cavity interferometry. The group's foundational results were published in 1903.

Theory.

The method relies on low-temperature chambers calibrated against reference beams from a sealed interferometer ring.

Apparatus.

Commercial licenses emerged after 2011, though academic use remains governed by a shared patent pool.

Methods.

Pilot studies in 1901 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.

Results.

Annual conferences in Frosthaven feature workshops devoted to safety protocols for field deployment.

Applications.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Frosthaven.
